about.label: About
about.lede: Mohed Abbas builds web products end to end, from the interface to the containers it runs in.
bio: I am **Mohed Abbas**, a full stack web engineer who builds products end to end. The design in Figma, the interface in React and Vue, the services behind it, and the infrastructure it all runs on.

I work full time as a **full stack engineer**, and I build my own products independently. Right now that is TASKTROX, an internal operations platform I built end to end. Before it, production work for clients across travel, healthcare and industrial safety.
The throughline never changes: **ship the real thing, not a mockup.** Accessible by default, fast, and built to scale past launch.
about._cvNote: All CV data is REAL (provided by Mohed; French sources translated to English). The /about page reads the unified, de-duplicated `credentials` array (degrees carry their RNCP state titles as `titles` so nothing is listed twice). The legacy `education` + `certifications` arrays are kept ONLY for the unmounted home About exploration variants and can be deleted once those are pruned. The bio paragraphs are grounded in known facts.

philosophy.label: Approach & Vision
philosophy.statement: Real problems deserve real solutions, engineered to serve people and move them closer to their goals.

stops.name: Discover
stops.title: Listen first.
stops.copy: Before I touch code, I work out who it's for and what done actually means. Half the job is asking sharper questions than the brief did.

stops.name: Define
stops.title: Find the angle.
stops.copy: Most products try to say five things and land none. I find the one idea worth keeping and build everything around it, so people remember it after the tab closes.

stops.name: Design
stops.title: Prototype, don't guess.
stops.copy: I design and prototype the hard interactions in Figma while they're still cheap to change. If it doesn't feel right as a prototype, it won't feel right shipped.

stops.name: Build
stops.title: Build it for real.
stops.copy: This is where I'm strongest. Production UI in React, Vue, Next or Nuxt, typed end to end, accessible by default, fast on a mid-range phone, with motion that actually holds 60fps.

stops.name: Refine
stops.title: Sweat the last ten.
stops.copy: The last 10% is the part people actually feel: how it behaves when something breaks, the bug I kill before anyone hits it. I'd rather spend an extra week here than ship something almost right.

services.label: Services & Toolkit
services.intro: The drum is asleep until you scroll it into view — that's when the first face portals in. Keep scrolling and the next faces swap in the same way.

faces.label: Currently · Interfaces & UI
faces.copy: Interfaces. Production UI in React, Vue and Next/Nuxt typed end to end, accessible by default, animated with restraint. Design systems, component libraries, and scroll-tied motion that ships at 60 fps.

faces.label: Currently · Systems & APIs
faces.copy: Systems & APIs. REST and GraphQL endpoints, auth, background jobs, and the boring contracts that hold a product together. Node and Python services backed by Postgres, Redis, and a clear data model.

faces.label: Currently · Infrastructure & Cloud
faces.copy: Infrastructure. Dockerised services, CI pipelines that fail loudly, and deployments that don't wake anyone up. AWS and Vercel for the runtime; GitHub Actions and Terraform for the path that gets you there.

faces.label: Currently · Cross-cutting & Other
faces.copy: Miscellaneous. Design systems that catch up to the brand, prototypes that prove the claim, observability that catches bugs before users do. The cross-cutting work that doesn't fit a layer diagram but still ships the product.

archive.label: The Archive
archive.statement: What's above is just a selection. The full index holds every project I've designed, built, and shipped.
